leads.issues: A site search of everything Google has indexed for acetypewriter.com turns up only two pages: the homepage and a single blog post ("Keyboard Deconstructed"). leads.issues: The homepage's indexed title tag is just "Welcome to Brown County Forge" — no service, no location, no keyword for Google to match against a search like "blacksmith classes Bloomington Indiana." Several inner pages swing the opposite direction: the Indiana University ironwork portfolio page's indexed title is "Hidden Ironwork - Indiana University Ironwork - Brown County Forge - Terran Marks the Blacksmith - Bloomington Indiana - Brown County Forge" (business name repeated twice at the end), and the knife-classes page follows the same stuffed, duplicated pattern. On top of that, the business is split across two separately indexed domains — browncountyforge.com and store.browncountyforge.com — dividing whatever search authority either one builds.
leads.opportunity: Rewriting the homepage title to lead with the actual service and location, cleaning up the stuffed/duplicated inner-page titles into single clear phrases, and mapping a plan to consolidate the store subdomain back under the main site is a fix that needs no new content — Terran already has the real proof points (1,500+ students taught since 2015, custom ironwork for Indiana University). Expected: better click-through on searches like "blacksmith classes Bloomington" and "custom house numbers Indiana" within 30-60 days, plus one domain building authority instead of two competing with each other.
leads.email_subject: Your homepage title just says 'Welcome', Terran
leads.email_body:
Hi Terran,

I was looking at Brown County Forge and searched a few things a customer might type - "blacksmith classes Bloomington Indiana," "custom house numbers Indiana" - and your homepage's title tag is just "Welcome to Brown County Forge." No service, no location, nothing for Google to match a search against.

Meanwhile some of your inner pages have the opposite problem - the Indiana University ironwork page title is stuffed with repeated keywords and ends with your business name twice in a row. And your shop is split across browncountyforge.com and store.browncountyforge.com, so any search authority you build gets divided between two domains instead of one.

None of that reflects what's actually true - 1,500+ students taught since 2015, real custom work for places like IU, a distinct hand-forged look. Google just doesn't have it yet.

Put together a quick page on what I'd fix and why: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-17-brown-county-forge.html

No pressure either way, just thought it was worth flagging since your actual track record is a lot stronger than what's showing up in search.

leads.issues: A Google index check for barnstudioceramics.co.uk shows the Hand & Footprints page indexed twice on two different addresses: the current https://barnstudioceramics.co.uk/hand-footprints and a leftover http://www.barnstudioceramics.co.uk/hand-footprints.html from an older version of the site — same content, competing with itself, with the old version not even running on a secure connection. The "How It Works" page shows the same pattern, with an old http:// URL indexed alongside the current one, suggesting a systematic leftover from the last site rebuild rather than one stray link.
leads.opportunity: Redirecting the old http:// / .html URLs to their current https equivalents is a single redirect rule, not a rebuild — the current site already has everything it needs. Expected within 2-4 weeks: the duplicate old-site pages drop out of Google's index, every visitor and all search authority lands on the secure, current version, and nobody hits a browser security warning on a leftover page.
leads.email_subject: Google's still indexing your old site alongside the new one, Amanda
leads.email_body:
Hi Amanda,

I was looking at Barn Studio Ceramics and checked how your Hand & Footprints page shows up on Google. It's actually indexed twice - the current version at barnstudioceramics.co.uk/hand-footprints, and an old leftover at http://www.barnstudioceramics.co.uk/hand-footprints.html from before your last site update. The old one isn't even on a secure connection, so someone could land on it and see a browser warning instead of your studio.

Your "How It Works" page has the same issue - an old http:// version still indexed right alongside the current one. Looks like a pattern from the last rebuild rather than a one-off.

Put together a page showing what a cleaned-up, single-URL version looks like: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-17-barn-studio-ceramics.html

More on how I work at https://organic-theory.vercel.app

No pressure either way, just thought 30+ years in Earlswood deserved one clean address online instead of two competing ones.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder
